Named only in the trip title/metadata; not present in the available diary text (A_0316_L). No supporting excerpt exists in this bundle, so the stop cannot be cited. Chaumard is a Morvan (Nievre) village, but this cannot be confirmed without the missing diary pages.

Named only in the trip title/metadata; not present in the available diary text (A_0316_L). No supporting excerpt exists in this bundle, so the stop cannot be cited. Cluny (Saone-et-Loire) is consistent with a Burgundy trip, but unverifiable from the supplied page.

Appears in the trip title as a distinct entry alongside Chaumard. May refer to the Morvan regional park generally (in which case Chaumard is the Morvan stop) or to a separate location. Not present in the available diary text; cannot distinguish or cite without the missing diary pages.

Peter is said to fly to Lyons (Lyon). It is unclear whether Lyon was a visit/stop for the main party or only Peter's arrival point; the main party presumably drove. No Lyon stop is confirmed in the available diary text.

Only the departure sentence is transcribed; all named stops (Vendeuil, Chaumard, Morvan, Cluny) derive from the trip title only and have no supporting page text, so they were moved to unresolved[]
summary: On Monday 29 July 2002, two days after returning from Thorperness, Simon, Eve, John and Charles set off by car for France, with Peter due to fly out to Lyon. The only diary text available in this bundle is the departure sentence on a boundary page; the trip's substantive pages were not transcribed, so the named stops (Vendeuil, Chaumard, Morvan, Cluny) cannot be cited and are flagged unresolved.
extraction_notes: Only one boundary page (A_0316_L) was supplied, capturing the trip's departure sentence. A grep of the entire bundle confirms that Vendeuil, Chaumard, Morvan and Cluny appear ONLY in the trip_id/title_guess metadata and NOT in any page text. Per the citation rule, every non-home main_route stop must have a real source_page_id and a verbatim excerpt; the title-only stops had neither, so they were moved to unresolved[] rather than kept uncited or fabricated. The retained main_route is home -> Dover -> Calais -> home: the Dover/Calais crossing terminals are cited from A_0316_L ("we have left for France") and follow the default England-France dover-calais inference. The closing home stop carries no citation as role=home is exempt. uncited_remaining = 0 (every non-home main_route/excursion stop now has source_page_id + excerpt). Re-extract when the trip's diary pages become available.
